data.title: SunCar Technology prices $50M follow-on share offering
data.description:
SunCar Technology Group (NASDAQ:SDA) priced its previously announced public offering of ~7.14M shares for total expected gross proceeds of ~$50M.

Underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional ~1.07M shares.

The offering is expected to close on or about February 7, 2025.

SunCar (NASDAQ:SDA) intends to use the net proceeds for working capital and general corporate purposes.

The stock price declined 4% on Wednesday during after-market hours of trading.

data.title: Siemens Healthineers Non-GAAP EPS of €0.51, revenue of €5.48B; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Siemens Healthineers AG press release (OTCPK:SEMHF): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of €0.51.
  • Revenue of €5.48B (+5.8% Y/Y).
  • Free cash flow reached around €810 million, more than three times as high as in the prior-year quarter.
  • Equipment order intake in the first quarter markedly exceeded equipment revenue. The equipment book-to-bill ratio was an excellent 1.21.
  • Adjusted EBIT rose 11% to around €822 million in the first quarter from the prior-year period.
  • Outlook: For fiscal year 2025, we continue to expect comparable revenue growth of between 5% and 6% over fiscal year 2024. The expectation for adjusted basic earnings per share remains unchanged in the range of €2.35 to €2.50.

data.title: Kesko Oyj targets 2025 operating profit of €640M-€740M amid strategic price cuts and acquisitions
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Kesko Oyj (OTCPK:KKOYF) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Jorma Rauhala highlighted Kesko's Q4 turnaround, marking the first quarterly result improvement in eight quarters. Net sales increased, with notable growth in Grocery Trade and Car Trade, while Building & Technical Trade faced historical lows. He emphasized that Kesko expanded its presence in Denmark through acquisitions, projecting a 20% market share in the Danish building and home improvement sector once finalized.

  • Rauhala announced a dividend proposal of €0.90 per share, representing an 81% payout ratio.

  • CFO Anu Hamalainen underscored Kesko's strong financial position, noting that interest-bearing net debt to EBITDA stood at 1.1, well below the target maximum of 2.5. She also elaborated on increased expenses due to acquisitions and real estate costs but highlighted effective cost control measures.

  • Ari Akseli, President of Grocery Trade, detailed the implementation of a €50M price reduction program covering 1,200 products, aiming to enhance competitiveness and customer traffic.

Outlook

  • Kesko projects a 2025 comparable operating profit of €640M-€740M, supported by an improving economic cycle.

  • Grocery Trade is expected to maintain operating margins above 6% despite the ongoing price investment program. Investments in the store network and quality enhancements remain central to the strategy.

  • Building & Technical Trade profitability is anticipated to improve as the construction cycle recovers, especially in Denmark and Poland.

  • Car Trade profitability is estimated to remain stable despite weak demand for new cars, with ongoing strength in used cars and service sales.

Financial Results

  • Q4 net sales exceeded €3B, marking a 1.1% comparable increase. Grocery Trade saw 1.2% growth, supported by strong Christmas sales and a 7.6% rise in online grocery sales.

  • Grocery Trade's operating margin for 2024 was 6.9%, with significant investments in price competitiveness and store updates. Kespro outperformed the market, gaining share with net sales up 2.5% in Q4.

  • Building & Technical Trade's Q4 performance aligned with expectations, with Denmark and Poland showing clear sales improvements. However, the division faced a €40M goodwill write-down in Norway due to weak market conditions.

  • Car Trade achieved a 9.7% increase in Q4 net sales, driven by strong Volkswagen model sales and growth in used cars and services.

Q&A

  • Maria Wikström, SEB, inquired about the guidance range's lower end. CEO Rauhala emphasized confidence in improving profitability and clarified that Danish acquisitions not yet completed were excluded from the guidance.

  • Miika Ihamaki, DNB Markets, questioned Building & Technical Trade's start to the year. Rauhala expressed satisfaction with January's performance, particularly in Finland, Denmark, and Poland, and noted expectations for continued growth.

  • Magnus Råman, Kepler Cheuvreux, sought details on price investment impacts. CFO Hamalainen assured that operational efficiencies and sales growth would offset margin pressures.

  • Rob Joyce, BNP Paribas Exane, asked about price competition in Grocery Trade. Akseli confirmed that price reductions improved customer traffic and were aligned with long-term goals.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on guidance clarity and competitive dynamics in Grocery Trade. Concerns about the impact of price investments and goodwill impairments in Norway indicated a slightly negative tone.

  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing strategic initiatives and positive market developments. Rauhala frequently reiterated confidence in improving profitability and market conditions.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's sentiment showed increased optimism due to Q4's positive performance and improving market trends.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2025 reflects a higher range (€640M-€740M) compared to 2024 (€630M-€680M), signaling confidence in improving profitability.

  • Strategic focus shifted further towards price competitiveness and quality enhancements in Grocery Trade, building on Q3's stable performance.

  • Analysts continued to scrutinize price investments, but concerns about Building & Technical Trade's recovery appeared less pressing due to visible improvements.

  • Management's tone was more positive than in Q3, driven by Q4's improved results and strategic progress.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified geopolitical tensions, consumer confidence, and interest rates as key uncertainties.

  • Analysts raised concerns about the potential impact of price investments on Grocery Trade margins and the ongoing challenges in Norway's construction market.

  • The €40M goodwill write-down in Norway highlighted risks in Building & Technical Trade, though management expressed confidence in future improvements.

Final Takeaway

Kesko reported a strong Q4 turnaround, supported by growth in Grocery Trade and Car Trade and strategic acquisitions in Denmark. The company remains confident in its 2025 outlook, with plans to invest €50M in price reductions and expand its store network. While challenges persist in Building & Technical Trade, especially in Norway, management's focus on operational efficiencies and strategic initiatives underscores its commitment to long-term growth and profitability. Investors should monitor the impact of price cuts and the integration of recent acquisitions closely.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Société Générale Société anonyme reports Q4 results; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Société Générale Société anonyme press release (OTCPK:SCGLY): FY revenues of EUR 26.8 billion, up by +6.7% vs. 2023, above the ≥+5% target set for 2024.
  • Cost-to-income ratio of 69.0%, below the target of <71% set for 2024.
  • Profitability (ROTE) of 6.9%, above the target of >6% expected for 2024.
  • CET1 ratio of 13.3% at end-2024, around 310 basis points.
  • 2025 FINANCIAL TARGETS, STRONG CAPITAL, EXECUTION DISCIPLINE: Revenue growth of more than +3% vs. 2024. Decrease in costs above -1% vs. 2024 Improvement of the cost-to-income ratio, less than 66% in 2025. Cost of risk between 25 and 30 basis points in 2025 Increase of the ROTE, more than 8% in 2025 CET1 ratio above 13% post Basel IV throughout the year 2025.

data.title: Ørsted A/S reports FY results; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Ørsted A/S press release (OTCPK:DNNGY): FY operating profit (EBITDA) for 2024 amounted to DKK 32.0 billion compared to DKK 18.7 billion in 2023.
  • EBITDA excluding new partnerships and cancellation fees amounted to DKK 24.8 billion, an increase of DKK 0.7 billion compared to 2023 and in line with our guidance of DKK 24-26 billion.
  • Profit for the year amounted to DKK 0.0 billion. Excluding impairments (after tax) and cancellation fees, profit for the year was DKK 6.4 billion.
  • Return on capital employed came in at 4.5 %. ROCE adjusted for impairment losses and cancellation fees was 10.1%.
  • Guidance: In 2025, EBITDA excluding new partnership agreements and cancellation fees is expected to be DKK 25-28 billion, and gross investments are expected to be DKK 50-54 billion.

data.title: Morgan Stanley-led banks sell $5.5B of X debt: stake in xAI, Musk-Trump alliance in play - reports
data.description:
A group of banks led by Morgan Stanley sold $5.5 billion of debt related to Elon Musk's acquisition of platform X, formerly known as Twitter, according to a Bloomberg report that cites people with knowledge of the matter.

The debt was part of the $13 billion loan that the banks lent to support Musk's $44 billion acquisition of Twitter in 2022. The loans sold had senior-most credits, while the banks still hold about $6 billion of X’s debt.

The banks initially intended to sell around $3 billion of the loans; however, they later increased the offering to $5.5 billion after stronger-than-expected investor interest. The banks were looking to sell it for at least 95 cents on the dollar, the report said.

In the end, investors, including Pimco and Citadel, agreed to buy the loans at 97 cents on the dollar, a WSJ report showed. These floating-rate debts carry an interest rate of about 11%.

Although the banks typically attempt to sell such loans soon after the deal is closed, investors likely balked after Musk swept changes to the platforms, laid off staff, and fended off advertisers, causing a sharp decline in revenue.

Interest was revived in platform X after Trump's election victory in November. As Elon Musk, the man behind EV giant Tesla (TSLA) took on a role as an adviser to the President, advertisers got back to the platform, the bankers pitching the debt said.

Investors are also optimistic about gaining exposure to Musk’s AI startup xAI, which is perceived as a potential growth driver. X financial documents showed that it holds a 10% stake in xAI, valued at around $5 billion, the people familiar with the matter said.

The banks included Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS), Bank of America (NYSE:BAC), Barclays (NYSE:BCS), Mitsubishi UFJ (NYSE:MUFG), BNP Paribas (OTCQX:BNPQF), Mizuho (NYSE:MFG), and Societe Generale (OTCPK:SCGLF).

data.title: Booz Allen spinout SnapAttack acquired by Cisco
data.description:
• Booz Allen Hamilton (NYSE:BAH) Wednesday said that Cisco has completed its acquisition of SnapAttack, the cyber threat hunting and detection company spun out of Booz Allen in 2021.
  • Booz Allen remained an investor in SnapAttack following the spinout.
  • “We spun out SnapAttack because we believe in the power of dual-use technology to speed outcomes,” said Booz Allen Chief Financial Officer Matt Calderone.

data.title: Bioceres Crop Solutions sees lower Q2 revenue amid headwinds in Argentina, stock down
data.description:
Bioceres (BIOX) Wednesday said that second quarter revenues are expected to be about $105 million, down 25% from a year ago, as Argentina continues to face significant headwinds related to weather and inventory overhang.

The company’s shares fell more than -6% in after-market trading.

Gross profit is expected to be $45 million with adjusted EBITDA of $15 million, also lower than a year ago.

Bioceres (BIOX) said the results reflect challenging conditions in Argentina, which remains a decisive market for the financial performance of the company in the first half of the year.

“Farmer purchasing behavior in the country changed significantly compared to the prior year, driven by macroeconomic conditions and unfavorable weather. This, together with high channel inventories, led to lower crop protection and micro-beaded fertilizer sales.”

Bioceres Crop Solutions will release its second quarter 2025 earnings on Tuesday, February 11, 2025, after the stock market close.

data.title: HIVE Digital mines 102 BTC in January, Bitcoin holdings up by 34% Y/Y
data.description:
• HIVE Digital Technologies (NASDAQ:HIVE) mined 102 BTC in January, bringing its HODL portfolio to 2,657 BTC, valued at $271 million as of January 31, the company said on Thursday.
  • The company's Bitcoin holdings increased by 34% year-over-year.
  • However, on a month-over-month basis, BTC holdings saw a decline as HIVE strategically sold a portion of its BTC to invest in capital assets, including Bitfarms' (BITF) 200 megawatt ("MW") hydro-powered Bitcoin mining assets in Paraguay.
  • HIVE Digital Technologies (NASDAQ:HIVE) achieved an average daily production rate of 3.3 BTC, equivalent to 18 BTC per Exahash.

data.title: Asia-Pacific markets rise, tracking gains on Wall Street amid cooling US-China trade tensions
data.description:
Asia-Pacific markets traded mostly in rise on Thursday tracking gains on Wall Street and driven by easing concerns over a global trade war, after the US and China adopted more restrained tariff measures, with US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping scheduled for a phone call soon.

Japan (NKY:IND) rose 0.58% to surpass 39,000, while the broader Topix Index rose 0.7% to 2,764 on Thursday, marking a third consecutive session of gains for Japanese stocks. The Japanese yen strengthened beyond 152 per dollar on Thursday, reaching its highest level in eight weeks, as Bank of Japan board member Naoki Tamura said that the central bank must lift the policy rate to at least 1% in the latter part of fiscal 2025.

Meanwhile, investors kept a close eye on the failed merger talks between Honda and Nissan, with Honda proposing an acquisition of Nissan instead.

China (SHCOMP) rose 0.83% to around 3,235 while the Shenzhen Component gained 0.6% to 10,225 on Thursday, recouping some losses from the previous session, with artificial intelligence and related technology stocks leading the charge.

Those gains were driven by enthusiasm around Chinese artificial intelligence startup DeepSeek, which is poised to challenge Western AI leaders. The offshore yuan steadied round 7.28 per dollar, as concerns over escalating U.S.-China trade tensions began to subside.

Investors are now focused on China's upcoming inflation data, which will offer further insights into the country's economic health.

Hong Kong (HSI) rose 0.61% to 20,690 on Thursday morning session, shifting from notable losses in the previous session as all sectors moved higher.

India (SENSEX) fell 0.22% to 78,094 in morning trade on Thursday, retreating for the second straight session, weighed down by consumer durables, real estate, and metals.

The Indian rupee (USD:INR) hit an all-time low of 87.54 on Thursday amid increased USD buying, driven by the maturity of non-deliverable forward positions, capital outflows, and investor risk aversion.

Traders remained cautious, although the RBI is expected to deliver an interest rate cut amid a slowdown in economic growth.

Australia (AS51) rose 1.23% to close at 8,521 on Thursday, recovering most of its earlier losses for the week as concerns over a global trade war eased. The Australian dollar held steady around $0.628 on Thursday, after rising for three consecutive sessions, supported by a weaker US dollar.

Meanwhile, data revealed that Australia’s trade surplus narrowed in December, as export growth slowed while imports rose at a faster pace.

In the U.S., on Wednesday, all three major indexes ended higher, buoyed by a sharp drop in long-term Treasury yields as investors digested a mix of earnings reports and economic data.

U.S. stock futures rise on Thursday following gains on Wall Street overnight during a week plagued by tariffs, disappointing tech earnings, and mixed US economic data: Dow +0.22%; S&P 500 +0.26%; Nasdaq +0.30%.

Looking ahead, investors are focusing on quarterly reports from Amazon and other major companies on Thursday, as well as the latest weekly jobless claims.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia:

  • China’s services sector growth slows to four-month low in January

  • Japan’s Jan services PMI revised upward to 53; real wages rises for 2nd consecutive month

  • Trump in 'no hurry' to speak to China's Xi Jinping over escalating trade tensions

  • Tit-for-Tat: China hits back with tariffs on U.S. coal and LNG, unveils Google probe

  • U.S. will delay Canada tariffs as countries work on beefing up border security
